text: THE PLACE OF THE PHILOSOPHY OF CHRISTIAN RELIGIOUS EDUCATION (CRE) IN THEORETICAL COLLEGES: A CRITIQUE This paper tries to show that Philosophy of CRE should be treated as a critical subject in the curriculum of theoretical colleges. It believes that philosophers are wiser than the religious adherents. Philosophy is highly valued for bringing clarity, intelligence and knowledge of man in general. This paper based on qualitative literature reviews tries to show that philosophical thinking has relation with the biblical theology. Wisdom of God is seen in the person of Jesus Christ, the Saviour of the world. Hence, the student of theology who learns philosophy will graduate far beyond from what the religious adherents can do in terms of having a “worldviewish” theology, and thereby capable of tackling and confronting all the worldly and secular issues and challenges. Therefore, this study argues that Philosophical issues like metaphysics and issues of knowledge (epistemology) should be grounded and buttressed with scripture, in order to make students of theology to appropriate the study of science to life, cultivate moral rectitude and thus effectively discharge their ecclesiastical duties. The study thus sees the study of philosophy of CRE as very critical and necessary for the shaping the minds of the Christian future clergies.
